You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). You have Bash, Read, Grep tools.

STEP 1 — get your file list. Run:
  /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; s=json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[11]; print(chr(10).join(s))"
That prints your assigned slice as lines of "relpath:START-END" (relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent).

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every one of your assigned files/ranges, line by line, using the Read tool with the given offset/limit. Do NOT grep-and-skim. Actually read the code so you catch names a keyword search would miss.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (module/file name, class, function/method, variable, constant, enum value) that would look UNPROFESSIONAL in an external code review. This includes:
  - marketing / quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, proper(when boastful), aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best, clean(when boastful)
  - temporal / version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(when it means 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes / informal / vague: hack, yolo, stuff, misc, foo, doit, thing, blah, magic numbers named as words
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME, not KEEP.** Only mark KEEP for names that are unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a real domain term (e.g. 'finalize', 'proper_noun', 'evict_older_than', a real product name like 'SmartArt' ONLY if the code truly implements it).

STEP 4 — WRITE your findings by APPENDING TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_11.tsv, one row per flagged name:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY_it_reads_unprofessional<TAB>SUGGESTED_professional_name
  (VERDICT = RENAME or KEEP-BUT-NOTED; KIND = file/class/func/var/const/enum)
Use a bash heredoc or printf with real tab characters. If a file range has NO issues, still write one row: NONE<TAB>-<TAB><relpath><TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-.

Return ONLY a one-line summary: "shard 11: <R> renames, <K> kept-noted across <F> files".

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. 1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-wire-013 (#1327): INDEPENDENT §-1.1 forensic render-audit (the trustworthy short test).
3	
4	A CLEAN-ROOM render-integrity detector. It imports ZERO production predicates — in particular it
5	does NOT call ``weighted_enrichment.is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable`` / ``key_findings.
6	is_truncated_fragment`` / ``evaluate_render_chrome_canary``. Those are the BLIND predicates this
7	test exists to cross-check: on the banked render the manual §-1.1 audit found ~85 chrome / ~35
8	truncation / 37 contradiction-noise units, while the production predicate (and so the canary AND
9	the v1 fast harness ``iwire013_fast_render_audit.py``) report ~0. The blindness has two roots and
10	this detector fixes both:
11	
12	  1. UNIT BLINDNESS — the canary only enumerates clean TOP-LEVEL ``- `` bullets, so it never
13	     sees chrome glued INSIDE the Abstract prose, the section bodies, or the 697-unit
14	     "Corroborated Weighted Findings" citation blob. This detector enumerates EVERY claim-bearing
15	     unit (Abstract prose, every ``- **…**`` Key-Findings bullet, every claim-section body, each
16	     header title line, AND every ``[N]``-split unit of the Corroborated Weighted Findings).
17	
18	  2. PREDICATE BLINDNESS — the production chrome check is a WHOLE-UNIT junk classifier that
19	     returns False the moment a unit also contains real prose ("…over the recent past.[1] 1
20	     Introduction 1.1 Research background 1.2 Resea.[14]" reads as real text). This detector uses
21	     CONTAINMENT forensic rules: a unit is chrome if it CONTAINS an author/ORCID/affiliation list,
22	     a license/open-access stub, bibliographic/portal junk, browser/UI junk, a glued markdown
23	     header / ToC fragment, or a non-Latin scrape block — even when wrapped in otherwise-real
24	     prose. Truncation is detected at the ``[N]`` boundary (cut word before/after a marker), NOT
25	     by an ellipsis marker (the production ``is_truncated_fragment`` only matches a trailing
26	     ``…``/``-`` and so misses every mid-word span cut in this report).
27	
28	KNOWN-WORD BASIS (the truncation precision key): the "is this boundary token a real word or a
29	span cut?" decision is grounded in the RUN'S OWN sources — every word that occurs >= floor times
30	across ``evidence_pool.json`` (``direct_quote`` / ``statement`` / ``title``) is "known". So
31	"labor", "demand", "Acemoglu", "polarization" land in the known set automatically (this corpus is
32	ABOUT labor) and never false-flag, while span-cut fragments ("Resea", "publica", "hodology") are
33	absent and flag. No embedded English dictionary; no network.
34	
35	FAIL-LOUD: exit non-zero when chrome > --chrome-max OR truncation > --truncation-max OR
36	contradiction-noise > --contradiction-noise-max. ABSENT INPUT IS A FAILURE, never a pass: a
37	missing report.md / evidence_pool.json / contradictions.json prints SKIPPED and forces a non-zero
38	exit (the §-1.1 false-green guard — the OPPOSITE of the v1 harness's "PASS (partial)").
39	
40	Scaffolding sections are excluded from the claim-unit set deliberately: ``Reliability header``,
41	``Methods``, ``Capability disclosures``, ``Contradiction disclosures``, ``Bibliography``,
42	``Source corroboration``, ``Evidence-support disclosure``. These are pipeline scaffolding, not
43	carried-up source prose — the Bibliography legitimately lists DOIs/URLs, so auditing it for
44	"bibliographic junk" would false-flag. Audited: the Abstract, the Key-Findings bullets, every
45	analytical ``###``/``####`` section body, the Corroborated Weighted Findings blob, and the
46	Conclusion.
47	
48	LAW VI: every threshold + path is a CLI arg / env read; the known-word floor is a CLI arg.
49	
50	Usage (LOCAL, offline, instant):
51	    python scripts/iwire013_sec11_forensic_audit.py
52	    python scripts/iwire013_sec11_forensic_audit.py --report outputs/<run>/report.md
53	"""
54	
55	from __future__ import annotations
56	
57	import argparse
58	import json
59	import re
60	import sys
61	from collections import Counter
62	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
63	from pathlib import Path
64	from typing import Any
65	
66	# Fail-loud thresholds (LAW VI; overridable). The banked render is EXPECTED to breach all three —
67	# FAIL on the banked report is the correct result; do not tune toward a pass.
68	_DEFAULT_CHROME_MAX = 5
69	_DEFAULT_TRUNCATION_MAX = 3
70	_DEFAULT_CONTRADICTION_NOISE_MAX = 0
71	_DEFAULT_KNOWN_WORD_FLOOR = 5
72	
73	# Section headers that are pipeline SCAFFOLDING, not carried-up source prose (excluded from the
74	# claim-unit set). Matched case-insensitively against the header title's leading text.
75	_SCAFFOLDING_TITLES = (
76	    "reliability header",
77	    "methods",
78	    "capability disclosures",
79	    "contradiction disclosures",
80	    "bibliography",
81	    "source corroboration",
82	    "evidence-support disclosure",
83	    "research report:",  # the H1 echo of the question prompt — not a claim
84	)
85	
86	_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]")
87	# One or more trailing ``[N]`` citation markers (optionally whitespace-separated) at the very end of
88	# a unit. Stripped from a Key-Findings bullet so the boundary-word check inspects the word BEFORE
89	# the citation, not the marker itself (Codex P1-2).
90	_TRAILING_MARKERS_RE = re.compile(r"(?:\s*\[\d+\])+\s*$")
91	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'\-]*[A-Za-z]|[A-Za-z]")
92	# A run of non-Latin script (Arabic / CJK) — the report is supposed to be English-only, so a block
93	# of these characters is a foreign-page scrape carried up as a "claim".
94	_NONLATIN_RE = re.compile(r"[؀-ۿݐ-ݿ一-鿿぀-ヿ가-힯]{4,}")
95	_ORCID_RE = re.compile(r"\b\d{4}-\d{4}-\d{4}-\d{3}[\dxX]\b")
96	# A numbered ToC / heading token, e.g. "1 Introduction", "1.1 Research background", "5.2 AI and".
97	_TOC_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(?:^|\s)\d+(?:\.\d+){0,3}\s+[A-Z][a-z]")
98	# A glued/inline markdown header inside a unit body (not a clean leading header).
99	_INLINE_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"(?:^|[^\n#])#{1,6}\s+[A-Za-z]")
100	# Author-with-superscript-affiliation list: "Kanbach1,2 · Louisa Heiduk1 · …" (middot separators).
101	_AFFIL_MIDDOT_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z]{2,}\d{1,2}(?:,\d)?\s*[·•]")
102	# Two-letter boundary tokens that are legitimate short words / abbreviations (never a cut).
103	_SHORT_OK = {
104	    "ai", "it", "is", "of", "to", "in", "on", "or", "an", "as", "be", "by", "we", "us", "no",
105	    "so", "do", "etc", "al", "eg", "ie", "vs", "id", "ml", "ui", "ux", "hr", "ev", "us", "uk",
106	    "eu", "gn", "io", "pp", "ed", "co", "re", "at", "if", "up", "my", "go", "he", "me", "ok",
107	}
108	
109	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
110	# I-deepfix-001 P1_chrome_gate (#1344) — CLEAN-ROOM detector mirror of the SEVEN box1 chrome
111	# CLASSES. INDEPENDENT by construction: these regexes/heuristics are authored SEPARATELY here and
112	# import NOTHING from weighted_enrichment (the module's line-4 zero-production-predicate contract).
113	# The whole point of the I-wire-013 clean-room yardstick is that shared code = shared blind spot;
114	# the detector must catch the SAME classes by its OWN path, and any divergence from the production
115	# predicate on a fresh class is a FEATURE (it surfaces production blindness), never a bug to be
116	# resolved by unifying the two implementations. Each rule targets the same class as the production
117	# rule but keys on a distinct set of signals.
118	#   (1) paywall / purchase CTA        (2) multilingual license / repository furniture
119	#   (3) glued author-stats-table      (4) asterisked-author street + ZIP affiliation
120	#   (5) exec / promo bio              (6) stitched metadata-recital citation
121	#   (7) short nav / topic-list stub (four-guarded, own stopword-density precision guard)
122	_DET_PAYWALL_CTA_RE = re.compile(
123	    r"add to (?:cart|basket)"
124	    r"|purchase (?:this |the )?(?:article|full[- ]text|pdf|access)"
125	    r"|buy (?:this )?(?:article|pdf|now)"
126	    r"|subscribe (?:for|to get) (?:unlimited|full|instant|immediate)"
127	    r"|rent (?:this )?article"
128	    r"|free trial"
129	    r"|printable version",
130	    re.IGNORECASE,
131	)
132	_DET_REPO_LICENSE_RE = re.compile(
133	    r"standard-?nutzungsbedingungen|nutzungsbedingungen|econstor|"
134	    r"leibniz-informationszentrum|die dokumente auf|"
135	    r"zu eigenen wissenschaftlichen zwecken",
136	    re.IGNORECASE,
137	)
138	_DET_STATS_TABLE_RE = re.compile(
139	    r"\bobs\.?\s+mean\b|\bmean\s+std\.?\s*dev\b|\bvariable\s+obs\b|\bstd\.?\s*dev\.?\s+min\s+max\b",
140	    re.IGNORECASE,
141	)
142	# Detector-owned surname-digit pair regex (authored separately from the production
143	# _SURNAME_DIGIT_PAIR_RE; equivalent semantics, own source): a >=3-letter name-like stem welded — or
144	# joined by ONE space — to a 1-2 digit superscript not part of a decimal / percent / longer number.
145	_DET_SURNAME_DIGIT_PAIR_RE = re.compile(r"\b([A-Za-z]{3,})[ ]?(\d{1,2})(?![\w.%])")
146	# Detector-owned author/affiliation co-signal (mirrors production _AUTHOR_COSIGNAL_RE, own regex):
147	# an affiliation keyword as a WHOLE word ("\bCollege\b" never matches the welded "College2"), an
148	# "et al." marker, or an email address. The superscript author asterisk is NOT a co-signal at all —
149	# not even the ">=2 starred pairs" form: two starred CATEGORY labels ("High School1* Low College2*
150	# earnings differed") are byte-identical to a two-author starred byline, so the iter-1 starred-pair
151	# heuristic over-stripped that real finding (Codex iter-2 P1 blocker). The exactly-2-pair upgrade now
152	# requires an INDEPENDENT author signal (affiliation keyword / "et al." / email); a bare-stars-only
153	# byline is an accepted leak (leaked furniture << deleting a real finding, §-1.3 precision-first).
154	_DET_AUTHOR_COSIGNAL_RE = re.compile(
155	    r"\b(?:university|institute|college|department)\b"
156	    r"|\bet\s+al\b"
157	    r"|[\w.+-]+@[\w-]+\.[a-z]{2,}",
158	    re.IGNORECASE,
159	)
160	_DET_POSTAL_BLOCK_RE = re.compile(r"\b[A-Z][A-Za-z.\-]+,\s+[A-Z]{2}\s+\d{5}(?:-\d{4})?\b")
161	_DET_CORRESPONDENCE_RE = re.compile(r"\*\s*correspond|correspond(?:ing|ence)\s+(?:author|to)", re.IGNORECASE)
162	_DET_STREET_RE = re.compile(
163	    r"\b\d{1,5}\s+(?:[A-Z][a-z]+\s+){1,4}"
164	    r"(?:street|st\.|avenue|ave\.?|road|rd\.?|drive|dr\.?|boulevard|blvd\.?|lane|way|square|court)\b",
165	    re.IGNORECASE,
166	)
167	_DET_EXEC_TITLE_RE = re.compile(
168	    r"chief\s+\w+\s+officer|\bc[efot]o\b|(?:co-)?founder|managing director|"
169	    r"president and ceo|vice president|executive director|partner at",
170	    re.IGNORECASE,
171	)
172	_DET_PROMO_RE = re.compile(
173	    r"visionary|thought leader|passionate about|award[- ]winning|world[- ]class|leading expert|"
174	    r"driving (?:digital )?transformation|renowned|seasoned (?:leader|executive|professional)|"
175	    r"trusted advisor|proven track record|industry veteran",
176	    re.IGNORECASE,
177	)
178	_DET_METADATA_RECITAL_RE = re.compile(
179	    r"journal article[^.]{0,80}volume\s+\d+[^.]{0,60}(?:article\s+\d+|authored by)"
180	    r"|volume\s+\d+[^.]{0,30}issue\s+\d+[^.]{0,30}authored by",
181	    re.IGNORECASE,
182	)
183	_DET_TRAILING_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"(?:\s*\[\d+\])+\s*$")
184	_DET_TRAILING_NUMBER_RE = re.compile(r"(?:^|\s)\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,2})?\s*$")
185	# Independent (detector-owned) stopword set — authored separately from the production
186	# _CHROME_STOPWORDS so the two yardsticks never share source.
187	_DET_STOPWORDS = frozenset(
188	    "the a an and or of to in on at for with by from as is are was were be been that this it its "
189	    "they we our you your which who than into over under about between among per not no so if while "
190	    "during after before up down out all each more most some such also may can will has have had "
191	    "do does did versus vs both".split()
192	)
193	
194	
195	def _det_stopword_density(text: str) -> float:
196	    """Detector-owned stopword density (fraction of alphabetic tokens that are stopwords)."""
197	    toks = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z]+", text.lower())
198	    return (sum(1 for t in toks if t in _DET_STOPWORDS) / len(toks)) if toks else 0.0
199	
200	
201	def _det_surname_digit_pairs(text: str) -> int:
202	    """Detector rule 3b: count "Surname<digit>" author-superscript pairs (welded 'Kanbach1' or single-
203	    space 'Archbold 2') by the detector-owned _DET_SURNAME_DIGIT_PAIR_RE. NO finding-label allowlist —
204	    the pair COUNT plus an author/affiliation co-signal (see the call site) separates a genuine byline
205	    from a real two-category finding, so 'High School1 Low College2 earnings differed' (2 pairs, no
206	    co-signal) is KEPT while a >=3-name (or 2-name + affiliation) byline still fires."""
207	    return len(_DET_SURNAME_DIGIT_PAIR_RE.findall(text))
208	
209	
210	def _det_is_titlecase_heading(text: str) -> bool:
211	    """Detector rule 7 helper: every content word (>=3 letters, not a stopword) starts uppercase — the
212	    Title-Case shape of a ToC / nav heading. Detector-owned; REPLACES the old finite-verb-absence
213	    heuristic (a real short claim carries a lowercase verb, so it is not Title-Case and is kept)."""
214	    content = [w for w in re.findall(r"[A-Za-z]+", text) if len(w) >= 3 and w.lower() not in _DET_STOPWORDS]
215	    return len(content) >= 2 and all(w[0].isupper() for w in content)
216	
217	
218	def _det_is_short_nav_item(text: str) -> bool:
219	    """Detector rule 7: a short ToC/nav/topic stub — <=6 words AND a bare trailing number AND stopword
220	    density below 0.10 AND Title-Case heading shape. Own precision guard, independent of production."""
221	    core = _DET_TRAILING_CITE_RE.sub("", text.strip()).strip()
222	    toks = core.split()
223	    if not (1 <= len(toks) <= 6):
224	        return False
225	    if not _DET_TRAILING_NUMBER_RE.search(core):
226	        return False
227	    if _det_stopword_density(core) >= 0.10:
228	        return False
229	    return _det_is_titlecase_heading(core)
230	
231	
232	def _det_box1_chrome_flags(text: str) -> list[str]:
233	    """Detector-owned flags for the seven box1 chrome classes (clean-room; imports no production
234	    predicate). Returns the list of class labels that fire."""
235	    out: list[str] = []
236	    if _DET_PAYWALL_CTA_RE.search(text):
237	        out.append("paywall_cta")
238	    if _DET_REPO_LICENSE_RE.search(text):
239	        out.append("repo_license")
240	    _det_pairs = _det_surname_digit_pairs(text)
241	    # Asterisk is NOT a co-signal (see _DET_AUTHOR_COSIGNAL_RE note): two starred category labels are
242	    # byte-identical to a starred byline, so the exactly-2-pair upgrade requires an INDEPENDENT author
243	    # signal only (Codex iter-2 P1 blocker). Precision-first per §-1.3.
244	    _det_cosignal = bool(_DET_AUTHOR_COSIGNAL_RE.search(text))
245	    if _DET_STATS_TABLE_RE.search(text) or (
246	        (_det_pairs >= 3 or (_det_pairs == 2 and _det_cosignal))
247	        and _det_stopword_density(text) < 0.10
248	    ):
249	        out.append("author_stats_table")
250	    if (
251	        _DET_POSTAL_BLOCK_RE.search(text)
252	        and (_DET_STREET_RE.search(text) or _DET_CORRESPONDENCE_RE.search(text))
253	    ) or (_DET_CORRESPONDENCE_RE.search(text) and _DET_STREET_RE.search(text)):
254	        out.append("affiliation_address")
255	    if _DET_EXEC_TITLE_RE.search(text) and _DET_PROMO_RE.search(text):
256	        out.append("exec_promo_bio")
257	    if _DET_METADATA_RECITAL_RE.search(text):
258	        out.append("metadata_recital")
259	    if _det_is_short_nav_item(text):
260	        out.append("short_nav_item")
261	    return out
262	
263	
264	@dataclass
265	class _Unit:
266	    """One claim-bearing unit. ``category`` is its provenance (abstract / key_finding / section /
267	    corroborated / header). ``ends_before_marker`` / ``starts_after_marker`` mark the [N] boundary
268	    sides eligible for a span-cut check."""
269	
270	    category: str
271	    text: str
272	    ends_before_marker: bool = False
273	    starts_after_marker: bool = False
274	    flags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
275	
276	
277	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
278	# Known-word basis (the run's OWN corpus) — fully offline, no embedded dictionary.
279	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
280	def build_known_words(snapshot_dir: Path, floor: int) -> tuple[set[str], int, bool]:
281	    """Every lowercase token occurring >= ``floor`` times across this run's fetched source text
282	    (``evidence_pool.json`` direct_quote/statement/title; ``corpus_snapshot.json`` text fields as a
283	    SUPPLEMENT). Returns (known_set, source_chars, evidence_pool_ok).
284	
285	    ``evidence_pool.json`` is the REQUIRED known-word basis: ``evidence_pool_ok`` is True iff it is
286	    present AND parseable. A missing / unreadable evidence_pool.json is FAIL-LOUD — the caller exits
287	    non-zero (SKIPPED -> FAIL for coverage) and the corpus_snapshot.json supplement is NOT consulted,
288	    so an absent required basis can never be silently masked into a false green (Codex P1-1). The
289	    supplement only fills in when the required basis is confirmed present+readable but yielded no
290	    usable text."""
291	    freq: Counter[str] = Counter()
292	    chars = 0
293	    ev = snapshot_dir / "evidence_pool.json"
294	    evidence_pool_ok = False
295	    if ev.is_file():
296	        try:
297	            rows = json.loads(ev.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) or []
298	            evidence_pool_ok = True  # present AND parseable -> the required known-word basis exists
299	            for r in rows:
300	                if not isinstance(r, dict):
301	                    continue
302	                for fkey in ("direct_quote", "statement", "title"):
303	                    t = r.get(fkey) or ""
304	                    if isinstance(t, str) and t:
305	                        chars += len(t)
306	                        for w in _WORD_RE.findall(t):
307	                            freq[w.lower()] += 1
308	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - present but malformed -> required basis UNREADABLE -> FAIL
309	            evidence_pool_ok = False
310	    # corpus_snapshot.json only SUPPLEMENTS once the required evidence_pool basis is confirmed
311	    # present+readable. A missing/unreadable evidence_pool MUST NOT be masked by this fallback —
312	    # that silent fall-through was the §-1.1 false-green hole (Codex P1-1).
313	    if chars == 0 and evidence_pool_ok:
314	        cs = snapshot_dir / "corpus_snapshot.json"
315	        if cs.is_file():
316	            try:
317	                blob = cs.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
318	                chars += len(blob)
319	                for w in _WORD_RE.findall(blob):
320	                    freq[w.lower()] += 1
321	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
322	                pass
323	    known = {w for w, c in freq.items() if c >= floor}
324	    return known, chars, evidence_pool_ok

330	def _is_scaffolding(title: str) -> bool:
331	    t = title.strip().lower().lstrip("# ").strip()
332	    return any(t.startswith(s) for s in _SCAFFOLDING_TITLES)
333	
334	
335	def enumerate_units(report_text: str) -> list[_Unit]:
336	    """Enumerate every claim-bearing unit in the rendered report:
337	
338	      * each header TITLE line (so a glued-chrome header — "# A Fourth Industrial Revolution
339	        Paradigm Shift… ## Dennis Zami Atibuni…" — is audited as its own unit, not silently
340	        consumed as a section title);
341	      * the Abstract prose, [N]-split (skipping the italic ``_…_`` disclaimer);
342	      * every ``- **…**`` Key-Findings bullet (one unit per bullet, continuation lines joined);
343	      * every claim-section ``###``/``####`` body, [N]-split (this is where the Corroborated
344	        Weighted Findings 697 units come from).
345	
346	    Scaffolding sections (Bibliography / Methods / disclosures / Reliability header) contribute
347	    NO body units, but their clean titles are still title-audited (and never trip a chrome rule)."""
348	    lines = report_text.split("\n")
349	    units: list[_Unit] = []
350	
351	    # Group the report into (title, level, body_lines) sections.
352	    sections: list[tuple[str, int, list[str]]] = []
353	    cur_title, cur_level, cur_body = "", 0, []
354	    header_re = re.compile(r"^(#{1,6})\s+(.*\S)\s*$")
355	    for ln in lines:
356	        m = header_re.match(ln)
357	        if m:
358	            if cur_title or cur_body:
359	                sections.append((cur_title, cur_level, cur_body))
360	            cur_title, cur_level, cur_body = m.group(2), len(m.group(1)), []
361	        else:
362	            cur_body.append(ln)
363	    if cur_title or cur_body:
364	        sections.append((cur_title, cur_level, cur_body))
365	
366	    for title, _level, body_lines in sections:
367	        # (1) the header title itself is a unit (catches glued-chrome titles).
368	        if title:
369	            units.append(_Unit("header", title))
370	        if _is_scaffolding(title):
371	            continue
372	        body = "\n".join(body_lines)
373	        low_title = title.strip().lower()
374	
375	        if low_title.startswith("key findings"):
376	            units.extend(_enumerate_key_findings_bullets(body))
377	            continue
378	
379	        # Abstract + analytical sections + corroborated + conclusion: drop the italic disclaimer
380	        # paragraph, then [N]-split the remaining prose into citation units.
381	        prose = _strip_italic_disclaimer(body)
382	        if not prose.strip():
383	            continue
384	        cat = "abstract" if low_title.startswith("abstract") else (
385	            "corroborated" if "corroborated weighted findings" in low_title else "section"
386	        )
387	        units.extend(_split_on_markers(prose, cat))
388	    return units
389

391	def _strip_italic_disclaimer(body: str) -> str:
392	    """Drop the leading ``_…_`` provenance-disclaimer paragraph (pipeline boilerplate, not a
393	    claim) so it is neither chrome-flagged nor truncation-flagged."""
394	    out_lines = []
395	    for ln in body.split("\n"):
396	        s = ln.strip()
397	        if s.startswith("_") and s.endswith("_") and len(s) > 2:
398	            continue
399	        out_lines.append(ln)
400	    return "\n".join(out_lines)

403	def _enumerate_key_findings_bullets(body: str) -> list[_Unit]:
404	    """One unit per ``- `` / ``- **…**`` bullet; continuation lines (a wrapped truncation tail)
405	    join into the same bullet."""
406	    units: list[_Unit] = []
407	    cur: list[str] | None = None
408	    for ln in body.split("\n"):
409	        s = ln.strip()
410	        if s.startswith("_") and s.endswith("_"):
411	            continue
412	        if s.startswith("- "):
413	            if cur is not None:
414	                units.append(_make_kf_unit("\n".join(cur)))
415	            cur = [s[2:]]
416	        elif cur is not None:
417	            cur.append(ln)
418	    if cur is not None:
419	        units.append(_make_kf_unit("\n".join(cur)))
420	    return units

423	def _make_kf_unit(text: str) -> _Unit:
424	    # A KF bullet ends right before its trailing [N] and starts a fresh claim -> both boundaries
425	    # are eligible for a span-cut check. Strip the trailing [N] citation marker(s) FIRST so the
426	    # end-cut check inspects the word right before the citation ("...restricted to s.[25]" -> "s"),
427	    # not the marker itself: keeping the [N] made _last_word land on "]" and silently miss every
428	    # cut-word-before-citation in a KF bullet (Codex P1-2).
429	    text = _TRAILING_MARKERS_RE.sub("", text)
430	    return _Unit("key_finding", text, ends_before_marker=True, starts_after_marker=True)
431	
432	
433	def _split_on_markers(prose: str, category: str) -> list[_Unit]:
434	    """Split prose on ``[N]`` citation markers into citation units. Every piece EXCEPT the last
435	    ends right before a marker; every piece EXCEPT the first starts right after one."""
436	    pieces = _MARKER_RE.split(prose)
437	    n = len(pieces)
438	    units: list[_Unit] = []
439	    for i, piece in enumerate(pieces):
440	        if not piece.strip():
441	            continue
442	        units.append(
443	            _Unit(
444	                category,
445	                piece,
446	                ends_before_marker=(i < n - 1),
447	                starts_after_marker=(i > 0),
448	            )
449	        )
450	    return units

454	# Chrome detection (containment forensic rules — independent of production)
455	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
456	def chrome_flags(text: str) -> list[str]:
457	    """Forensic chrome categories a unit CONTAINS (not whole-unit junk). Returns the list of
458	    category labels that fire; non-empty => the unit is chrome."""
459	    s = text
460	    low = s.lower()
461	    flags: list[str] = []
462	
463	    # browser / UI junk
464	    if (
465	        "refresh the page or clear your browser cache" in low
466	        or "clear your browser cache" in low
467	        or re.search(r"\bclose\s*[-–]\s*share\b", low)
468	        or "most recent answer" in low
469	        or "i need some assistance" in low
470	        or "download associated records" in low
471	    ):
472	        flags.append("browser_ui")
473	
474	    # license / open access
475	    if (
476	        "creative commons" in low
477	        or "creativecommons.org/licenses" in low
478	        or "open access article distributed under" in low
479	        or re.search(r"©\s*20\d\d\s*the authors", low)
480	        or "this is an open access article" in low
481	    ):
482	        flags.append("license")
483	
484	    # author / ORCID / affiliation / submission metadata
485	    if (
486	        _ORCID_RE.search(s)
487	        or "orcid" in low
488	        or _AFFIL_MIDDOT_RE.search(s)
489	        or re.search(r"received:\s*\d", low)
490	        or re.search(r"accepted:\s*\d", low)
491	        or "published online" in low
492	        or re.search(r"©\s*the author", low)
493	    ):
494	        flags.append("author_meta")
495	
496	    # bibliographic / portal junk (HARD markers only — a lone URL is NOT chrome)
497	    if (
498	        re.search(r"\bdoi:\s*10\.\d", low)
499	        or re.search(r"\bissn[a-z]?\b\s*:?\s*\d", low)
500	        or "crossref reports the following articles citing" in low
501	        or "volume title publisher" in low
502	        or len(re.findall(r"https?://", low)) >= 3
503	        or len(re.findall(r"https?://(?:dx\.)?doi\.org/", low)) >= 2
504	    ):
505	        flags.append("biblio_junk")
506	
507	    # glued markdown header / ToC fragment
508	    toc_hits = len(_TOC_TOKEN_RE.findall(s))
509	    if _INLINE_HEADER_RE.search(s) or toc_hits >= 2:
510	        flags.append("glued_header_toc")
511	
512	    # non-Latin / foreign-page scrape
513	    if _NONLATIN_RE.search(s):
514	        flags.append("nonlatin_scrape")
515	
516	    # I-deepfix-001 P1_chrome_gate (#1344): the seven box1 render-seam chrome classes, caught by the
517	    # detector's OWN independent path (clean-room mirror of the production predicate).
518	    flags.extend(_det_box1_chrome_flags(s))
519	
520	    return flags

523	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
524	# Truncation detection (span cut at the [N] boundary — no ellipsis marker needed)
525	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
526	def _last_word(text: str) -> tuple[str, bool]:
527	    """The trailing alphabetic word and whether it was immediately followed by a single period
528	    (the artificial '.' a span-truncator appends). Trailing hyphen/quote stripped."""
529	    s = text.rstrip().rstrip('"”\')')
530	    had_period = s.endswith(".")
531	    if had_period:
532	        s = s[:-1].rstrip()
533	    m = re.search(r"([A-Za-z][A-Za-z'\-]*)$", s)
534	    if not m:
535	        return "", had_period
536	    return m.group(1).strip("-'"), had_period
537	
538	
539	def _first_word(text: str) -> str:
540	    m = re.match(r"\s*([A-Za-z][A-Za-z'\-]*)", text)
541	    return m.group(1).strip("-'") if m else ""
542	
543	
544	# Suffixes that make a longer known word a mere INFLECTION of the token (so the token is the real
545	# base word, not a span cut): 'disadvantage' -> {'disadvantaged','disadvantages'} only -> NOT a cut.
546	# A real END cut has a non-inflectional completion ('resea' -> 'research' = 'resea'+'rch').
547	_INFLECTIONS = ("s", "d", "es", "ed", "ing", "ly", "ic")
548	
549	
550	def _has_longer_known_prefix(w: str, known: set[str]) -> bool:
551	    """True iff some KNOWN corpus word is ``w`` + a NON-inflectional tail (``w`` is a chopped-END
552	    prefix: 'resea' -> 'research'). A token whose only longer completions are inflections
553	    ('disadvantage' -> 'disadvantaged'/'disadvantages') is the real base word and returns False."""
554	    for k in known:
555	        if len(k) > len(w) and k.startswith(w) and k[len(w):] not in _INFLECTIONS:
556	            return True
557	    return False
558	
559	
560	def _has_longer_known_suffix(w: str, known: set[str]) -> bool:
561	    """True iff some KNOWN corpus word ENDS with ``w`` and is longer (``w`` is a chopped-START
562	    suffix: 'hodology' -> 'methodology', 'usand' -> 'thousand')."""
563	    return any(len(k) > len(w) and k.endswith(w) for k in known)
564	
565	
566	def _token_is_cut(token: str, known: set[str], *, mode: str) -> bool:
567	    """A boundary token is a span cut iff it is NOT a known corpus word AND it is a strict
568	    prefix (end-cut) / suffix (start-cut) of a LONGER known corpus word. The completion gate is
569	    what keeps precision high: a legit-but-rare sentence-ender ('classifier', 'computerisation')
570	    is either known or has no longer known completion, so it does NOT flag; a real span cut
571	    ('Resea'->'research', 'publica'->'publications') always does. len-1 before a marker is a cut
572	    by construction; len-2 keeps an abbreviation allowlist."""
573	    if not token:
574	        return False
575	    t = token.lower()
576	    if t in known:
577	        return False
578	    completes = _has_longer_known_prefix(t, known) if mode == "end" else _has_longer_known_suffix(t, known)
579	    if len(t) == 1:
580	        return t not in {"a", "i"}
581	    if len(t) == 2:
582	        return t not in _SHORT_OK and completes
583	    return completes  # len>=3 and a chopped fragment of a known corpus word -> a span cut
584	
585	
586	def truncation_flag(unit: _Unit, known: set[str]) -> str | None:
587	    """Return a short reason if the unit is a mid-word span cut at a [N] boundary, else None."""
588	    if unit.ends_before_marker:
589	        w, _had = _last_word(unit.text)
590	        if _token_is_cut(w, known, mode="end"):
591	            return f"end-cut:{w!r}"
592	    if unit.starts_after_marker:
593	        w = _first_word(unit.text)
594	        # only a LOWERCASE leading token is a mid-word continuation cut (an uppercase start is a
595	        # legitimate new sentence after the citation).
596	        if w and w[0].islower() and _token_is_cut(w, known, mode="start"):
597	            return f"start-cut:{w!r}"
598	    return None

602	# Contradiction-noise (deterministic, from contradictions.json)
603	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
604	def _claim_values(row: dict) -> list[float]:
605	    vals: list[float] = []
606	    for c in row.get("claims") or []:
607	        if isinstance(c, dict) and isinstance(c.get("value"), (int, float)):
608	            vals.append(float(c["value"]))
609	    return vals

612	def contradiction_noise(contradictions_path: Path | None, report_text: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
613	    """Count possible_metric_mismatch rows from contradictions.json AND confirm they render in the
614	    report. Each such row is §-1.1 noise: year-numbers / DOI / page / ISSN compared as if they were
615	    a metric, ``close/share`` non-metric subjects, or an empty-LHS range."""
616	    rendered = sum(
617	        1 for ln in report_text.split("\n")
618	        if ln.lstrip().startswith("- ") and "[possible_metric_mismatch]" in ln
619	    )
620	    if contradictions_path is None or not contradictions_path.is_file():
621	        return {"validated": False, "count": 0, "rendered_lines": rendered, "examples": []}
622	    try:
623	        rows = json.loads(contradictions_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) or []
624	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
625	        return {"validated": False, "count": 0, "rendered_lines": rendered, "examples": []}
626	
627	    pmm: list[dict] = []
628	    for r in rows:
629	        if not isinstance(r, dict):
630	            continue
631	        pred = str(r.get("predicate") or "")
632	        action = str(r.get("recommended_action") or "")
633	        if "possible_metric_mismatch" in pred or "metric mismatch" in action.lower():
634	            pmm.append(r)
635	
636	    examples: list[str] = []
637	    for r in pmm:
638	        subj = str(r.get("subject") or "").strip()
639	        pred = str(r.get("predicate") or "").strip()
640	        vals = _claim_values(r)
641	        why = []
642	        if any(abs(v) >= 1900 and abs(v) <= 2100 and float(v).is_integer() for v in vals):
643	            why.append("year-as-metric")
644	        if subj.lower() in {"close", "market", "paper"} or "/ share" in f"{subj} / {pred}":
645	            why.append("non-metric-subject")
646	        if not vals or any(v == 0 for v in vals):
647	            why.append("empty/zero-LHS")
648	        lo = min(vals) if vals else None
649	        hi = max(vals) if vals else None
650	        examples.append(f"{subj} / {pred}: range {lo} to {hi}" + (f"  [{','.join(why)}]" if why else ""))
651	    # noise = rows that actually RENDER in the report (the ship-affecting tally). Do NOT fall back
652	    # to len(pmm): pmm rows the render gate correctly suppressed are NOT shipped noise, so counting
653	    # them would false-FAIL a report whose headline block is already clean (Codex P2).
654	    count = rendered
655	    return {"validated": True, "count": count, "rendered_lines": rendered,
656	            "pmm_rows": len(pmm), "examples": examples}
657

662	def run_audit(report_text: str, snapshot_dir: Path, known: set[str], known_chars: int,
663	              contradictions_path: Path | None) -> dict[str, Any]:
664	    units = enumerate_units(report_text)
665	
666	    chrome_units: list[_Unit] = []
667	    for u in units:
668	        fl = chrome_flags(u.text)
669	        if fl:
670	            u.flags = fl
671	            chrome_units.append(u)
672	
673	    trunc_units: list[_Unit] = []
674	    truncation_validated = bool(known)
675	    if truncation_validated:
676	        for u in units:
677	            r = truncation_flag(u, known)
678	            if r:
679	                u.flags = (u.flags or []) + [r]
680	                trunc_units.append(u)
681	
682	    contra = contradiction_noise(contradictions_path, report_text)
683	
684	    return {
685	        "total_units": len(units),
686	        "chrome": {"count": len(chrome_units), "units": chrome_units, "validated": True},
687	        "truncation": {
688	            "count": len(trunc_units), "units": trunc_units,
689	            "validated": truncation_validated, "known_words": len(known), "known_chars": known_chars,
690	        },
691	        "contradiction": contra,
692	    }
693

697	    for u in units[:n]:
698	        snippet = " ".join(u.text.split())[:150]
699	        out.append(f"[{u.category}|{','.join(u.flags)}] {snippet}")
700	    return out

737	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
738	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="I-wire-013 independent §-1.1 forensic render-audit")
739	    parser.add_argument("--report", type=Path,
740	                        default=Path("outputs/iwire013_validate_local/report.md"),
741	                        help="rendered report.md to audit (sidecars resolved from its parent dir)")
742	    parser.add_argument("--chrome-max", type=int, default=_DEFAULT_CHROME_MAX)
743	    parser.add_argument("--truncation-max", type=int, default=_DEFAULT_TRUNCATION_MAX)
744	    parser.add_argument("--contradiction-noise-max", type=int, default=_DEFAULT_CONTRADICTION_NOISE_MAX)
745	    parser.add_argument("--known-word-floor", type=int, default=_DEFAULT_KNOWN_WORD_FLOOR)
746	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
747	
748	    report_path = args.report.resolve()
749	    snapshot_dir = report_path.parent
750	    print(f"[forensic] report={report_path}")
751	
752	    if not report_path.is_file():
753	        # NEVER pass on absent input: SKIPPED == FAIL-for-coverage (the §-1.1 false-green guard).
754	        print(f"[forensic] SKIPPED: report not found at {report_path}")
755	        print("[forensic] OVERALL: FAIL (input absent -> zero coverage)")
756	        return 2
757	
758	    report_text = report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
759	    known, known_chars, evidence_pool_ok = build_known_words(snapshot_dir, args.known_word_floor)
760	    if not evidence_pool_ok:
761	        # NEVER pass when the REQUIRED known-word basis is absent: a missing / unreadable
762	        # evidence_pool.json is SKIPPED == FAIL-for-coverage, never a silent corpus_snapshot.json
763	        # fallback to a false green (the §-1.1 false-green guard — Codex P1-1).
764	        print(f"[forensic] SKIPPED: required evidence_pool.json missing or unreadable in {snapshot_dir}")
765	        print("[forensic] OVERALL: FAIL (required known-word basis absent -> zero truncation coverage)")
766	        return 2
767	    contradictions_path = snapshot_dir / "contradictions.json"
768	    contradictions_path = contradictions_path if contradictions_path.is_file() else None
769	
770	    audit = run_audit(report_text, snapshot_dir, known, known_chars, contradictions_path)
771	    print_table(audit, args)
772	
773	    failures: list[str] = []
774	    if audit["chrome"]["count"] > args.chrome_max:
775	        failures.append(f"chrome={audit['chrome']['count']}>{args.chrome_max}")
776	    if not audit["truncation"]["validated"]:
777	        failures.append("truncation=SKIPPED(no known-word basis)")
778	    elif audit["truncation"]["count"] > args.truncation_max:
779	        failures.append(f"truncation={audit['truncation']['count']}>{args.truncation_max}")
780	    if not audit["contradiction"]["validated"]:
781	        failures.append("contradiction-noise=SKIPPED(contradictions.json absent)")
782	    elif audit["contradiction"]["count"] > args.contradiction_noise_max:
783	        failures.append(f"contradiction_noise={audit['contradiction']['count']}>{args.contradiction_noise_max}")
784	
785	    if failures:
786	        print(f"\n[forensic] OVERALL: FAIL ({'; '.join(failures)})")
787	        return 1
788	    print("\n[forensic] OVERALL: PASS (chrome + truncation + contradiction-noise all within bounds)")
789	    return 0
790	
791	
792	if __name__ == "__main__":
793	    raise SystemExit(main())

1	"""I-wire-014 CWF header diagnostic (offline, fast).
2	
3	Runs the REAL per-basket corroboration-header selection chain from
4	``run_honest_sweep_r3.py`` against a run's ``bibliography.json`` and reports,
5	for every basket, which screen leg rejected ``claim_text`` and what header was
6	finally chosen. Lets us iterate FIX-A (render the prose, don't dump hashes)
7	without a full replay.
8	
9	Usage:  python scripts/iwire014_cwf_header_diagnostic.py <bibliography.json>
10	"""
11	import json
12	import re
13	import sys
14	
15	from src.tools.access_bypass import clean_fetch_body, is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional
16	
17	# Import the exact header helpers used by the real render (post-FIX-A #1334).
18	from scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 import (  # type: ignore
19	    _normalize_claim_summary,
20	    _title_has_named_chrome,
21	    _claim_header_is_unrenderable,
22	    _best_corroboration_header,
23	    cwf_header_prose_enabled,
24	)
25	
26	_HASH_RE = re.compile(r"^clm_[0-9a-f]+$")
27	
28	
29	def _final_header(basket: dict, statement: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
30	    """Replicate the REAL header selection (run_honest_sweep_r3 :2195-2225, post-FIX-A).
31	    Returns (final_header_text, classification)."""
32	    ccid = str(basket.get("claim_cluster_id") or "")
33	    raw = str(basket.get("claim_text") or "")
34	    dechromed = clean_fetch_body(raw).cleaned_text if raw else ""
35	    claim = _normalize_claim_summary(dechromed, quote_trim=160)
36	    if (
37	        not claim
38	        or is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional(claim)
39	        or _title_has_named_chrome(claim)
40	        or _claim_header_is_unrenderable(raw)
41	    ):
42	        prose = _best_corroboration_header(basket, statement) if cwf_header_prose_enabled() else ""
43	        if prose:
44	            return prose, "FIXA_prose_or_title"
45	        subj = str(basket.get("subject") or "").strip()
46	        pred = str(basket.get("predicate") or "").strip()
47	        claim = (f"{subj} {pred}".strip()) or ccid
48	        if claim == ccid or _HASH_RE.match(claim):
49	            return claim, "HASH(clm_)"
50	        return claim, "2WORD_STUB(subject+predicate)"
51	    return claim, "claim_text_direct"
52	
53	
54	def main() -> None:
55	    path = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "bibliography.json"
56	    bib = json.load(open(path, encoding="utf-8"))
57	    entries = bib if isinstance(bib, list) else bib.get("bibliography", [])
58	    seen: set[str] = set()
59	    classes: dict[str, int] = {}
60	    samples: dict[str, list] = {}
61	    for b in entries:
62	        statement = str(b.get("statement") or "")
63	        for basket in (b.get("baskets") or []):
64	            ccid = str(basket.get("claim_cluster_id") or "")
65	            if not ccid or ccid in seen:
66	                continue
67	            seen.add(ccid)
68	            header, cls = _final_header(basket, statement)
69	            classes[cls] = classes.get(cls, 0) + 1
70	            samples.setdefault(cls, [])
71	            if len(samples[cls]) < 6:
72	                samples[cls].append((ccid[:18], repr(header[:140])))
73	    total = len(seen)
74	    bad = classes.get("HASH(clm_)", 0) + classes.get("2WORD_STUB(subject+predicate)", 0)
75	    print(f"unique baskets (deduped by ccid): {total}")
76	    print(f"REAL HEADER (sentence / title / claim_text): {total - bad}")
77	    print(f"JUNK HEADER (hash / 2-word stub): {bad}   <-- FIX-A target: 0\n")
78	    print("--- classification breakdown ---")
79	    for cls, n in sorted(classes.items(), key=lambda kv: -kv[1]):
80	        print(f"  {n:4d}  {cls}")
81	    print("\n--- samples per class (final header) ---")
82	    for cls, ss in samples.items():
83	        print(f"\n[{cls}]")
84	        for ccid, txt in ss:
85	            print(f"   {ccid}  {txt}")
86	
87	
88	if __name__ == "__main__":
89	    main()
90

1	#!/usr/bin/env python
2	"""I-wire-014 quantified replay harness (#1336).
3	
4	Reproduce the Phase-7 quantified SILENT NO-OP (firing_status=spec_validation_rejected)
5	on the BANKED drb_72 corpus, WITHOUT a full sweep. Two modes:
6	
7	  --writer   : ONE billed Writer (spec_provider) call on the VM (GLM-5.2). Banks the
8	               raw_spec JSON to <out>/raw_spec.json so all downstream iteration is offline.
9	  (default)  : load <out>/raw_spec.json (banked) and run build_quantified_spec +
10	               run_quantified_section OFFLINE with on_reject capturing the exact gate.
11	
12	LAW II: real banked corpus, no synthetic data. LAW VI: paths/model via args/env.
13	SPEND: --writer is the ONLY billed step (one call); the offline mode is spend-free.
14	"""
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import argparse
18	import asyncio
19	import json
20	import os
21	import sys
22	
23	# repo root on path
24	sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
25	
26	from src.polaris_graph.tools.evidence_extractor import extract_numbers_from_evidence
27	
28	
29	def _load_evidence_pool(corpus_dir: str) -> dict[str, dict]:
30	    """Load the banked evidence_pool.json (a list) into the {ev_id: row} dict the
31	    quantified path expects (mirrors run_honest_sweep_r3.py _q_ev_pool build)."""
32	    path = os.path.join(corpus_dir, "evidence_pool.json")
33	    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
34	        rows = json.load(fh)
35	    if isinstance(rows, dict):
36	        rows = list(rows.values())
37	    pool = {
38	        ev["evidence_id"]: ev
39	        for ev in rows
40	        if isinstance(ev, dict) and ev.get("evidence_id")
41	    }
42	    return pool
43	
44	
45	def _load_question(corpus_dir: str) -> str:
46	    snap = os.path.join(corpus_dir, "corpus_snapshot.json")
47	    with open(snap, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
48	        d = json.load(fh)
49	    return d.get("question", "")
50	
51	
52	async def _writer_call(question: str, sourced: list[dict], out_dir: str) -> dict | None:
53	    """Replicate the run_honest_sweep_r3.py _q_spec_provider Writer call EXACTLY, then
54	    bank both the raw response text and the parsed raw_spec for offline iteration."""
55	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
56	        OpenRouterClient,
57	        PG_GENERATOR_MODEL,
58	    )
59	    from scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 import parse_quantified_spec_response
60	
61	    shortlist = [
62	        {"evidence_id": d.get("evidence_id"), "label": d.get("label"),
63	         "context": d.get("context"), "value": d.get("value"),
64	         "unit": d.get("unit")}
65	        for d in sourced[:40]
66	    ]
67	    prompt = (
68	        "You are modeling a quantified trade-off for a research "
69	        "report. Using ONLY the sourced numbers below, emit a "
70	        "SINGLE JSON object (no prose) with keys model_id, title, "
71	        "inputs, outputs, sensitivity, solve_for per the POLARIS "
72	        "ModelSpec schema. Each SOURCED input MUST carry "
73	        "datapoint_ref:{ev_id,label,context,value,unit} copied "
74	        "EXACTLY from one listed number; mark every ASSUMPTION "
75	        "input modeled:true with base+unit+sweep. Every output "
76	        "formula must be pure arithmetic over the declared input "
77	        "names. If the numbers do not support a defensible model, "
78	        'return {"model_id":"none"} and nothing else.\n\n'
79	        f"QUESTION: {question}\n\n"
80	        f"SOURCED NUMBERS (JSON): {json.dumps(shortlist)[:8000]}"
81	    )
82	    max_tokens = int(os.environ.get("PG_QUANTIFIED_SPEC_MAX_TOKENS", "32768"))
83	    reasoning_max = int(os.environ.get("PG_QUANTIFIED_SPEC_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS", "8192"))
84	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=PG_GENERATOR_MODEL)
85	    try:
86	        resp = await client.generate(
87	            prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens, temperature=0.0,
88	            reasoning_max_tokens=reasoning_max,
89	            response_format={"type": "json_object"},
90	        )
91	    finally:
92	        await client.close()
93	    txt = getattr(resp, "content", "") or ""
94	    if not txt.strip():
95	        txt = getattr(resp, "reasoning", "") or ""
96	    os.makedirs(out_dir, exist_ok=True)
97	    with open(os.path.join(out_dir, "writer_raw_text.txt"), "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
98	        fh.write(txt)
99	    with open(os.path.join(out_dir, "writer_shortlist.json"), "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
100	        json.dump(shortlist, fh, indent=2)
101	    raw_spec = parse_quantified_spec_response(txt, sourced_count=len(sourced))
102	    with open(os.path.join(out_dir, "raw_spec.json"), "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
103	        json.dump(raw_spec, fh, indent=2)
104	    print(f"[writer] model={PG_GENERATOR_MODEL} content_chars={len(txt)} "
105	          f"raw_spec={'dict' if isinstance(raw_spec, dict) else raw_spec}")
106	    return raw_spec
107	
108	
109	def _offline_build(question: str, sourced: list[dict], pool: dict, out_dir: str) -> None:
110	    """Run build_quantified_spec on the banked raw_spec with on_reject capture."""
111	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import build_quantified_spec
112	
113	    raw_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "raw_spec.json")
114	    with open(raw_path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
115	        raw_spec = json.load(fh)
116	    if not isinstance(raw_spec, dict):
117	        print(f"[offline] raw_spec is not a dict ({raw_spec!r}) -> declined_no_spec")
118	        return
119	    reasons: list[str] = []
120	    spec = build_quantified_spec(
121	        question, sourced, pool,
122	        spec_llm=lambda _q, _s: raw_spec,
123	        on_reject=reasons.append,
124	    )
125	    print(f"[offline] inputs in raw_spec: {len(raw_spec.get('inputs', []))}")
126	    print(f"[offline] outputs in raw_spec: {len(raw_spec.get('outputs', []))}")
127	    if spec is None:
128	        print(f"[offline] REJECTED. reason(s): {reasons}")
129	    else:
130	        print(f"[offline] SPEC BUILT. model_id={spec.model_id} "
131	              f"sourced={len(spec.sourced_inputs)} modeled={len(spec.modeled_inputs)} "
132	              f"outputs={len(spec.outputs)}")
133	
134	
135	async def _offline_section(question: str, pool: dict, out_dir: str) -> None:
136	    """Run the full run_quantified_section using the banked raw_spec as the provider."""
137	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.quantified_analysis import run_quantified_section
138	
139	    raw_path = os.path.join(out_dir, "raw_spec.json")
140	    with open(raw_path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
141	        raw_spec = json.load(fh)
142	
143	    async def _provider(_q, _s):
144	        return raw_spec
145	
146	    section_md, telem = await run_quantified_section(
147	        question, pool, spec_provider=_provider, run_dir=out_dir,
148	    )
149	    print(f"[section] firing_status={telem.get('firing_status')} "
150	          f"quantified_status={telem.get('quantified_status')} "
151	          f"spec_reject_reason={telem.get('spec_reject_reason')} "
152	          f"verified_sentences={telem.get('verified_sentences')} "
153	          f"spec_produced={telem.get('spec_produced')}")
154	    if section_md:
155	        print("=== SECTION MD ===")
156	        print(section_md[:2000])
157	    with open(os.path.join(out_dir, "section_telem.json"), "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
158	        json.dump(telem, fh, indent=2, default=str)
159	
160	
161	def main() -> None:
162	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
163	    ap.add_argument("--corpus-dir", required=True)
164	    ap.add_argument("--out-dir", required=True)
165	    ap.add_argument("--writer", action="store_true",
166	                    help="make the ONE billed Writer call and bank raw_spec.json")
167	    args = ap.parse_args()
168	
169	    pool = _load_evidence_pool(args.corpus_dir)
170	    question = _load_question(args.corpus_dir)
171	    sourced = extract_numbers_from_evidence(pool)
172	    print(f"[harness] evidence rows={len(pool)} sourced_numbers={len(sourced)} "
173	          f"question[:60]={question[:60]!r}")
174	    os.makedirs(args.out_dir, exist_ok=True)
175	    # bank the extracted sourced numbers for offline label/context inspection
176	    with open(os.path.join(args.out_dir, "sourced_numbers.json"), "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
177	        json.dump(sourced[:60], fh, indent=2)
178	
179	    if args.writer:
180	        asyncio.run(_writer_call(question, sourced, args.out_dir))
181	        return
182	
183	    _offline_build(question, sourced, pool, args.out_dir)
184	    asyncio.run(_offline_section(question, pool, args.out_dir))
185	
186	
187	if __name__ == "__main__":
188	    main()
189

1	#!/usr/bin/env python
2	"""I-wire-016 (#1338) — OFFLINE pre-wiring acceptance gate for the render-seam predicate.
3	
4	This is the §-1.3 precision=1.0 acceptance test. It is NOT a wiring step: it imports the
5	PRODUCTION render-seam predicate ``is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable`` exactly as the render
6	seam calls it, runs it over three labelled sets, and decides PASS/FAIL.
7	
8	Sets
9	----
10	  * ``accept_chrome.json``      — POSITIVES (real page-furniture / chrome). Must be WITHHELD
11	                                  (flagged True). Drives ``chrome_recall``.
12	  * ``accept_truncation.json``  — POSITIVES (mid-word / cut-span truncations). Must be WITHHELD
13	                                  (flagged True). Drives ``truncation_recall``.
14	  * ``accept_content.json``     — NEGATIVES (real research findings). Must be KEPT (flagged
15	                                  False). Drives CONTENT-PRECISION.
16	
17	Each item is a ``{"text": str, "class": str}`` row. ``class`` is informational; the set a row
18	belongs to is determined by which file it loads from (positives vs negatives), so the gate is
19	robust to label noise inside the positives.
20	
21	The §-1.3 gate
22	--------------
23	  PASS iff  CONTENT-PRECISION == 1.0  (ZERO real findings flagged — the over-strip law: deleting a
24	            real finding is worse than leaking chrome)
25	       AND  ``chrome_recall`` and ``truncation_recall`` are both REPORTED (computed, not skipped).
26	
27	The truncation leg in the predicate is corpus-grounded. With ``known_words=None`` (no evidence
28	basis) the boundary-cut leg no-ops safely and only the unambiguous-marker leg runs — exactly the
29	render-seam contract when no corpus vocabulary is available. We therefore call the predicate with
30	an EMPTY/None known-words basis built via ``build_known_words_from_evidence([])`` (returns an empty
31	set), proving the truncation leg degrades safely. This is intentional: the offline gate has no run
32	corpus, so it exercises the marker-only truncation signal, which must never over-flag real prose.
33	
34	Usage
35	-----
36	    python scripts/iwire016_acceptance_test.py
37	
38	Exit code 0 == PASS, 1 == FAIL (so CI / a wiring gate can consume it). DOES NOT wire anything.
39	"""
40	from __future__ import annotations
41	
42	# Standard Library
43	import json
44	import sys
45	from pathlib import Path
46	
47	# Ensure the repo root is importable when this script is run directly
48	# (``python scripts/iwire016_acceptance_test.py`` puts ``scripts/`` on sys.path, not the root).
49	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
50	if str(_REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
51	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))
52	
53	# Local Modules
54	from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (  # noqa: E402
55	    build_known_words_from_evidence,
56	    is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable,
57	)
58	
59	_AUDIT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "outputs" / "audits" / "iwire016"
60	_CHROME_FILE = _AUDIT_DIR / "accept_chrome.json"
61	_TRUNCATION_FILE = _AUDIT_DIR / "accept_truncation.json"
62	_CONTENT_FILE = _AUDIT_DIR / "accept_content.json"
63	
64	
65	def _load_units(path: Path) -> list[dict]:
66	    """Load a labelled acceptance set (list of ``{text, class}`` rows). Raises FileNotFoundError if
67	    the file is absent (LAW II — fail loudly; a missing positive set must NOT silently pass the gate)
68	    and ValueError if the shape is wrong."""
69	    if not path.exists():
70	        raise FileNotFoundError(
71	            f"acceptance set missing: {path} (the gate cannot run without it — a sibling task "
72	            f"builds the positive sets; build them before running this gate)"
73	        )
74	    raw = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
75	    if not isinstance(raw, list):
76	        raise ValueError(f"{path}: expected a JSON list of {{text, class}} rows, got {type(raw)!r}")
77	    units: list[dict] = []
78	    for i, row in enumerate(raw):
79	        if not isinstance(row, dict) or "text" not in row:
80	            raise ValueError(f"{path}[{i}]: each row must be a dict with a 'text' field, got {row!r}")
81	        units.append(row)
82	    return units
83	
84	
85	def _flagged(units: list[dict], known_words: "set[str] | None") -> list[bool]:
86	    """Apply the PRODUCTION render-seam predicate to every unit. ``True`` == WITHHELD (chrome /
87	    truncation / unrenderable); ``False`` == KEPT (real renderable finding)."""
88	    return [
89	        is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable(u["text"], known_words=known_words)
90	        for u in units
91	    ]
92	
93	
94	def main() -> int:
95	    # The render-seam contract with no run corpus: an empty/None known-words basis. The truncation
96	    # boundary-cut leg no-ops safely (only the unambiguous-marker leg runs), so a real finding is
97	    # never flagged by a missing-corpus span guess.
98	    known_words = build_known_words_from_evidence([])  # -> empty set, == safe None-equivalent basis
99	    known_basis = known_words or None  # pass None when empty so the boundary leg is fully inert
100	
101	    chrome_units = _load_units(_CHROME_FILE)
102	    truncation_units = _load_units(_TRUNCATION_FILE)
103	    content_units = _load_units(_CONTENT_FILE)
104	
105	    # POSITIVES: must be WITHHELD (flagged True).
106	    chrome_flags = _flagged(chrome_units, known_basis)
107	    truncation_flags = _flagged(truncation_units, known_basis)
108	    # NEGATIVES: must be KEPT (flagged False).
109	    content_flags = _flagged(content_units, known_basis)
110	
111	    n_chrome = len(chrome_units)
112	    n_truncation = len(truncation_units)
113	    n_content = len(content_units)
114	
115	    chrome_recall = (sum(chrome_flags) / n_chrome) if n_chrome else 0.0
116	    truncation_recall = (sum(truncation_flags) / n_truncation) if n_truncation else 0.0
117	    # CONTENT-PRECISION = fraction of content (negative) units NOT flagged.
118	    content_kept = sum(1 for f in content_flags if not f)
119	    content_precision = (content_kept / n_content) if n_content else 0.0
120	
121	    # The real findings the predicate WRONGLY withheld (the §-1.3 violations).
122	    false_positives = [
123	        content_units[i]["text"]
124	        for i, f in enumerate(content_flags)
125	        if f
126	    ]
127	
128	    print("=" * 78)
129	    print("I-wire-016 (#1338) — render-seam predicate OFFLINE acceptance gate (§-1.3)")
130	    print("=" * 78)
131	    print(f"  chrome positives     : {n_chrome:4d}  flagged(withheld)={sum(chrome_flags):4d}"
132	          f"   chrome_recall      = {chrome_recall:.4f}")
133	    print(f"  truncation positives : {n_truncation:4d}  flagged(withheld)={sum(truncation_flags):4d}"
134	          f"   truncation_recall  = {truncation_recall:.4f}")
135	    print(f"  content negatives    : {n_content:4d}  kept(not flagged)={content_kept:4d}"
136	          f"   CONTENT-PRECISION  = {content_precision:.4f}")
137	    print("-" * 78)
138	
139	    precision_ok = content_precision == 1.0
140	    recalls_reported = (n_chrome > 0) and (n_truncation > 0)
141	
142	    if false_positives:
143	        print(f"  §-1.3 VIOLATION: {len(false_positives)} real finding(s) WRONGLY withheld "
144	              f"(content-precision must be 1.0):")
145	        for txt in false_positives[:20]:
146	            print(f"    - {txt[:140]}")
147	        if len(false_positives) > 20:
148	            print(f"    ... and {len(false_positives) - 20} more")
149	
150	    if not recalls_reported:
151	        print("  RECALLS NOT REPORTED: a positive set is empty — chrome_recall and "
152	              "truncation_recall must both be computed over non-empty sets.")
153	
154	    passed = precision_ok and recalls_reported
155	    print("-" * 78)
156	    print(f"  RESULT: {'PASS' if passed else 'FAIL'}  "
157	          f"(content_precision==1.0: {precision_ok}; recalls_reported: {recalls_reported})")
158	    print("=" * 78)
159	    return 0 if passed else 1
160	
161	
162	if __name__ == "__main__":
163	    sys.exit(main())
164

1	#!/usr/bin/env python
2	"""I-wire-016 #1338: element-level chrome/content classifier bake-off.
3	
4	Picks the render-seam furniture-vs-content classifier by benchmarking candidates on labeled gold:
5	content = real VERIFIED claim sentences; chrome = the §3.4-missed furniture/metadata/truncation gold.
6	
7	§-1.3 ACCEPTANCE GATE: a classifier may be WIRED only if its CONTENT precision is >= --content-precision-floor
8	(default 0.99) — i.e. it almost NEVER labels a real claim as chrome (that would be a faithfulness DROP).
9	Among classifiers that clear that floor, the winner maximizes CHROME recall (how much furniture it removes).
10	
11	Candidates (VM, GPU + OpenRouter):
12	  C1 embed-logreg : Qwen3-Embedding-8B embeddings + LogisticRegression head (stratified 5-fold CV).
13	  C2 embed-knn    : same embeddings, k-NN (k=5) cosine vote (5-fold CV).
14	  C3 glm-fewshot  : GLM-5.2 zero/few-shot LLM-as-classifier (conservative prompt: flag only obvious
15	                    furniture/metadata, KEEP anything that states a finding) — no training, eval on a sample.
16	
17	Run on the VM after a run frees the GPU:
18	  PYTHONPATH=/root/polaris /opt/conda/bin/python scripts/iwire016_chrome_classifier_bakeoff.py \
19	    --content outputs/audits/iwire014/benchmark/clf_content_set.json \
20	    --chrome  outputs/audits/iwire014/benchmark/chrome_gold.json \
21	    --out     outputs/audits/iwire016/bakeoff_result.json
22	"""
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import argparse
26	import json
27	import os
28	from pathlib import Path
29	from typing import Any
30	
31	
32	def _load_texts(path: Path) -> list[str]:
33	    raw = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
34	    if isinstance(raw, dict):
35	        raw = raw.get("items", raw.get("texts", list(raw.values())))
36	    out: list[str] = []
37	    for it in raw:
38	        if isinstance(it, str):
39	            t = it
40	        elif isinstance(it, dict):
41	            t = it.get("text") or it.get("sentence") or it.get("unit") or ""
42	        else:
43	            t = ""
44	        t = (t or "").strip()
45	        if len(t.split()) >= 3:
46	            out.append(t)
47	    return out
48	
49	
50	def _metrics(y_true: list[int], y_pred: list[int]) -> dict[str, float]:
51	    # label 1 = chrome (positive for "furniture removal"); 0 = content.
52	    tp = sum(1 for t, p in zip(y_true, y_pred) if t == 1 and p == 1)
53	    fp = sum(1 for t, p in zip(y_true, y_pred) if t == 0 and p == 1)
54	    tn = sum(1 for t, p in zip(y_true, y_pred) if t == 0 and p == 0)
55	    fn = sum(1 for t, p in zip(y_true, y_pred) if t == 1 and p == 0)
56	    chrome_recall = tp / (tp + fn) if (tp + fn) else 0.0
57	    chrome_prec = tp / (tp + fp) if (tp + fp) else 0.0
58	    # CONTENT precision = of the units we KEEP (pred=0/content), how many are truly content.
59	    content_precision = tn / (tn + fn) if (tn + fn) else 0.0
60	    return {
61	        "content_precision": round(content_precision, 4),  # THE §-1.3 gate (keep no chrome? no — keep all real content)
62	        "chrome_recall": round(chrome_recall, 4),
63	        "chrome_precision": round(chrome_prec, 4),
64	        "fn_real_content_flagged_as_chrome": fp,  # the §-1.3 danger count
65	        "tp": tp, "fp": fp, "tn": tn, "fn": fn,
66	    }
67	
68	
69	def _embed(texts: list[str]) -> Any:
70	    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
71	    from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer  # noqa: PLC0415
72	    model_id = os.getenv("PG_EMBED_MODEL_ID", "Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B")
73	    # fp16: the 8B embedder is ~23GB in fp32 and OOMs a 24GB GPU on the final alloc; fp16 ~16GB fits.
74	    m = SentenceTransformer(
75	        model_id, device="cuda", model_kwargs={"torch_dtype": torch.float16},
76	    )
77	    return m.encode(texts, batch_size=8, show_progress_bar=False, normalize_embeddings=True)
78	
79	
80	def _run_embed_candidates(content: list[str], chrome: list[str]) -> dict[str, Any]:
81	    import numpy as np  # noqa: PLC0415
82	    from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression  # noqa: PLC0415
83	    from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold  # noqa: PLC0415
84	    from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier  # noqa: PLC0415
85	
86	    texts = content + chrome
87	    y = [0] * len(content) + [1] * len(chrome)
88	    X = np.asarray(_embed(texts))
89	    y_arr = np.asarray(y)
90	    skf = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=0)
91	    out: dict[str, Any] = {}
92	    for name, mk in (
93	        ("embed-logreg", lambda: LogisticRegression(max_iter=2000, class_weight="balanced")),
94	        ("embed-knn", lambda: KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=5, metric="cosine")),
95	    ):
96	        yt, yp = [], []
97	        for tr, te in skf.split(X, y_arr):
98	            clf = mk(); clf.fit(X[tr], y_arr[tr])
99	            yp.extend(int(v) for v in clf.predict(X[te]))
100	            yt.extend(int(v) for v in y_arr[te])
101	        out[name] = _metrics(yt, yp)
102	    return out
103	
104	
105	def _run_glm_fewshot(content: list[str], chrome: list[str], sample: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
106	    import random  # noqa: PLC0415 — eval sampling only; seeded
107	    rnd = random.Random(0)
108	    cs = rnd.sample(content, min(sample, len(content)))
109	    hs = rnd.sample(chrome, min(sample, len(chrome)))
110	    texts = cs + hs
111	    y = [0] * len(cs) + [1] * len(hs)
112	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
113	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=os.getenv("PG_JUDGE_MODEL", "z-ai/glm-5.2"))
114	    sys_prompt = (
115	        "You screen one text unit from a research report. Label it FURNITURE if it is page chrome / "
116	        "boilerplate / bibliographic or author metadata / article-access stats / a navigation or license "
117	        "fragment / a mid-word-truncated fragment — i.e. NOT a substantive research finding. Label it "
118	        "CONTENT if it states a substantive claim/finding (even briefly). When in doubt, answer CONTENT "
119	        "(never drop a real finding). Answer with EXACTLY one word: FURNITURE or CONTENT."
120	    )
121	    yp = []
122	    for t in texts:
123	        try:
124	            r = client.complete_sync(system=sys_prompt, prompt=t[:1200]) if hasattr(client, "complete_sync") else None
125	            txt = (r.content if r is not None else "")
126	        except Exception:
127	            txt = ""
128	        yp.append(1 if "FURNITURE" in (txt or "").upper() else 0)
129	    return {"glm-fewshot": _metrics(y, yp)}
130	
131	
132	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
133	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
134	    ap.add_argument("--content", required=True, type=Path)
135	    ap.add_argument("--chrome", required=True, type=Path)
136	    ap.add_argument("--out", required=True, type=Path)
137	    ap.add_argument("--content-precision-floor", type=float, default=0.99)
138	    ap.add_argument("--glm-sample", type=int, default=80)
139	    ap.add_argument("--skip-glm", action="store_true")
140	    a = ap.parse_args(argv)
141	
142	    content = _load_texts(a.content)
143	    chrome = _load_texts(a.chrome)
144	    print(f"[bakeoff] content={len(content)} chrome={len(chrome)}")
145	    results: dict[str, Any] = {}
146	    results.update(_run_embed_candidates(content, chrome))
147	    if not a.skip_glm:
148	        try:
149	            results.update(_run_glm_fewshot(content, chrome, a.glm_sample))
150	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
151	            results["glm-fewshot"] = {"error": str(exc)[:200]}
152	
153	    floor = a.content_precision_floor
154	    eligible = {k: v for k, v in results.items() if isinstance(v, dict) and v.get("content_precision", 0) >= floor}
155	    winner = max(eligible, key=lambda k: eligible[k]["chrome_recall"], default=None)
156	    summary = {
157	        "content_precision_floor": floor,
158	        "results": results,
159	        "eligible_at_floor": list(eligible),
160	        "winner": winner,
161	        "winner_metrics": eligible.get(winner) if winner else None,
162	    }
163	    a.out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
164	    a.out.write_text(json.dumps(summary, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
165	    print(json.dumps(summary, indent=2))
166	    print(f"\n[bakeoff] WINNER (content_precision>={floor}, max chrome_recall): {winner}")
167	    return 0
168	
169	
170	if __name__ == "__main__":
171	    raise SystemExit(main())
172

1	#!/usr/bin/env python
2	"""I-wire-016 #1338 Phase-2: fine-tune Qwen3-0.6B as a content-vs-furniture classifier.
3	
4	Trains a binary sequence classifier (0=content, 1=furniture) on the cleaned labels, then
5	THRESHOLD-TUNES to the §-1.3 operating point: the SMALLEST P(furniture) threshold at which CONTENT
6	precision on the held-out test set is >= --content-precision-floor (default 0.99 — we must almost
7	never flag a real finding as furniture), and reports the chrome-recall achieved there. The model +
8	the chosen threshold are saved for wiring.
9	
10	VM: PYTHONPATH=/root/polaris /opt/conda/bin/python scripts/iwire016_finetune_chrome_classifier.py \
11	      --data outputs/audits/iwire016/clean_labeled.json \
12	      --out  outputs/audits/iwire016/classifier
13	``--data`` is a JSON list of {"text": str, "label": "content"|"furniture"}.
14	"""
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import argparse
18	import json
19	import os
20	from pathlib import Path
21	
22	import numpy as np
23	
24	
25	def _load(path: Path):
26	    rows = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
27	    texts, labels = [], []
28	    for r in rows:
29	        t = (r.get("text") or "").strip()
30	        lab = r.get("label")
31	        if len(t.split()) >= 3 and lab in ("content", "furniture"):
32	            texts.append(t)
33	            labels.append(1 if lab == "furniture" else 0)
34	    return texts, labels
35	
36	
37	def _threshold_for_precision(probs_furniture, y_true, floor: float):
38	    """Smallest threshold s.t. CONTENT precision (of the kept=content predictions) >= floor.
39	    Returns (threshold, content_precision, chrome_recall, chrome_precision, fp_real_content)."""
40	    best = None
41	    for thr in [i / 100 for i in range(50, 100)]:  # 0.50..0.99
42	        pred = [1 if p >= thr else 0 for p in probs_furniture]
43	        tp = sum(1 for t, p in zip(y_true, pred) if t == 1 and p == 1)
44	        fp = sum(1 for t, p in zip(y_true, pred) if t == 0 and p == 1)
45	        tn = sum(1 for t, p in zip(y_true, pred) if t == 0 and p == 0)
46	        fn = sum(1 for t, p in zip(y_true, pred) if t == 1 and p == 0)
47	        content_prec = tn / (tn + fn) if (tn + fn) else 1.0
48	        chrome_recall = tp / (tp + fn) if (tp + fn) else 0.0
49	        chrome_prec = tp / (tp + fp) if (tp + fp) else 0.0
50	        if content_prec >= floor:
51	            cand = (thr, round(content_prec, 4), round(chrome_recall, 4), round(chrome_prec, 4), fp)
52	            # smallest threshold that clears the floor (= max recall at-or-above the floor)
53	            if best is None:
54	                best = cand
55	            # keep the first (smallest thr) that clears; higher thr only raises precision/lowers recall
56	            break
57	    return best
58	
59	
60	def main(argv=None) -> int:
61	    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
62	    from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split  # noqa: PLC0415
63	    from transformers import (  # noqa: PLC0415
64	        AutoModelForSequenceClassification,
65	        AutoTokenizer,
66	        Trainer,
67	        TrainingArguments,
68	    )
69	
70	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
71	    ap.add_argument("--data", required=True, type=Path)
72	    ap.add_argument("--out", required=True, type=Path)
73	    ap.add_argument("--model-id", default=os.getenv("PG_CLF_BASE", "Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B"))
74	    ap.add_argument("--epochs", type=float, default=4.0)
75	    ap.add_argument("--content-precision-floor", type=float, default=0.99)
76	    ap.add_argument("--max-len", type=int, default=256)
77	    a = ap.parse_args(argv)
78	
79	    texts, labels = _load(a.data)
80	    print(f"[finetune] examples={len(texts)} content={labels.count(0)} furniture={labels.count(1)}")
81	    tr_t, te_t, tr_y, te_y = train_test_split(
82	        texts, labels, test_size=0.2, random_state=0, stratify=labels,
83	    )
84	
85	    tok = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(a.model_id, trust_remote_code=True)
86	    if tok.pad_token is None:
87	        tok.pad_token = tok.eos_token
88	    # Load in fp32 (the 0.6B model is ~2.4GB) — fp16 AMP (TrainingArguments fp16=True) keeps a fp32
89	    # master copy and handles mixed precision; loading the weights themselves in fp16 breaks the AMP
90	    # grad-unscale ("Attempting to unscale FP16 gradients").
91	    model = AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained(
92	        a.model_id, num_labels=2, trust_remote_code=True,
93	    )
94	    model.config.pad_token_id = tok.pad_token_id
95	
96	    class DS(torch.utils.data.Dataset):
97	        def __init__(self, t, y):
98	            self.enc = tok(t, truncation=True, max_length=a.max_len, padding="max_length")
99	            self.y = y
100	        def __len__(self): return len(self.y)
101	        def __getitem__(self, i):
102	            return {
103	                "input_ids": torch.tensor(self.enc["input_ids"][i]),
104	                "attention_mask": torch.tensor(self.enc["attention_mask"][i]),
105	                "labels": torch.tensor(self.y[i]),
106	            }
107	
108	    args = TrainingArguments(
109	        output_dir=str(a.out / "_ckpt"),
110	        num_train_epochs=a.epochs,
111	        per_device_train_batch_size=8,
112	        per_device_eval_batch_size=16,
113	        learning_rate=2e-5,
114	        weight_decay=0.01,
115	        logging_steps=20,
116	        save_strategy="no",
117	        report_to=[],
118	        fp16=True,
119	    )
120	    trainer = Trainer(model=model, args=args, train_dataset=DS(tr_t, tr_y))
121	    trainer.train()
122	
123	    # Test-set probabilities for P(furniture).
124	    model.eval()
125	    probs = []
126	    with torch.no_grad():
127	        for i in range(0, len(te_t), 16):
128	            batch = tok(te_t[i:i + 16], truncation=True, max_length=a.max_len,
129	                        padding=True, return_tensors="pt").to(model.device)
130	            logits = model(**batch).logits.float()
131	            p = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)[:, 1].cpu().numpy()
132	            probs.extend(p.tolist())
133	
134	    best = _threshold_for_precision(probs, te_y, a.content_precision_floor)
135	    # also report the default-0.5 operating point for context
136	    pred05 = [1 if p >= 0.5 else 0 for p in probs]
137	    tp = sum(1 for t, p in zip(te_y, pred05) if t == 1 and p == 1)
138	    fp = sum(1 for t, p in zip(te_y, pred05) if t == 0 and p == 1)
139	    tn = sum(1 for t, p in zip(te_y, pred05) if t == 0 and p == 0)
140	    fn = sum(1 for t, p in zip(te_y, pred05) if t == 1 and p == 0)
141	
142	    a.out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
143	    model.save_pretrained(str(a.out / "model"))
144	    tok.save_pretrained(str(a.out / "model"))
145	    result = {
146	        "model_id": a.model_id,
147	        "n_train": len(tr_t), "n_test": len(te_t),
148	        "at_0.5": {"content_precision": round(tn / (tn + fn), 4) if (tn + fn) else 1.0,
149	                   "chrome_recall": round(tp / (tp + fn), 4) if (tp + fn) else 0.0,
150	                   "fp_real_content": fp},
151	        "content_precision_floor": a.content_precision_floor,
152	        "tuned_operating_point": (
153	            {"threshold": best[0], "content_precision": best[1], "chrome_recall": best[2],
154	             "chrome_precision": best[3], "fp_real_content_flagged": best[4]}
155	            if best else None
156	        ),
157	    }
158	    (a.out / "result.json").write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
159	    print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))
160	    if best:
161	        print(f"\n[finetune] §-1.3 operating point: threshold={best[0]} content_precision={best[1]} "
162	              f"chrome_recall={best[2]} (real-content wrongly flagged={best[4]})")
163	    else:
164	        print(f"\n[finetune] NO threshold reaches content_precision>={a.content_precision_floor} — "
165	              "needs more/cleaner data or a different model.")
166	    return 0
167	
168	
169	if __name__ == "__main__":
170	    raise SystemExit(main())
